preamble: The debuccalization of a voiceless uvular stop to a glottal fricative is observed in various Arabic dialects and other Afroasiatic languages.

examples.note: In some urban dialects, /q/ debuccalizes, though [ʔ] is more common, [h] occurs in related processes.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
